blocks.text: You are a senior code reviewer. Review the ZCrystal Plugin codebase at /home/snow/.openclaw/workspace/zcrystal-plugin. Focus on: 1) Architectural issues 2) Code quality problems 3) Potential bugs 4) Performance concerns 5) Suggestions for cleanup. Examine src/index.ts, src/honcho-client.ts, src/tools/, src/self-evolution.ts, src/skill-manager.ts. Provide a concise JSON report with keys: critical_issues[], improvements[], working_well[], priority_fixes[].
I'll examine the codebase systematically. Let me start by reading the key files in parallel.
Now I have a thorough picture of the entire codebase. Let me compile the review.

blocks.input: {"critically_finding": "Reviewing ZCrystal Plugin - examining architectural issues, code quality, bugs, performance, and cleanup opportunities across src/index.ts, src/honcho-client.ts, src/tools/, src/self-evolution.ts, src/skill-manager.ts", "critical_issues": "[{\"severity\": \"critical\", \"file\": \"src/index.ts:177-204\", \"issue\": \"setInterval timers never cleaned up on plugin unregister/reload — heartbeat (5min) and proactive (10min) intervals accumulate across hot-reloads or re-registrations, holding stale state references and continuing to fire indefinitely.\", \"example\": \"setInterval(async () => { if (state) { ... } }, 5 * 60 * 1000) — no clearInterval on teardown path\"}, {\"severity\": \"critical\", \"file\": \"src/index.ts:60-69\", \"issue\": \"Module-level `let state: PluginState | null` singleton — re-calling register() creates a new state and orphans the old one (with its running timers and in-flight async ops), causing resource leaks and stale closures in all tool handlers that capture state.\", \"example\": \"state = { router, honcho, ... } overwrites previous without cleanup; new tool closures capture new state while old intervals still reference stale state.\"}, {\"severity\": \"critical\", \"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:375-385, 640-662\", \"issue\": \"Fire-and-forget IIFE for backup creation in commitEvolution and applyBestCandidate — runs detached with `.catch()` swallowing errors; if the subsequent skill write fails, the backup may not have been written yet, leaving no rollback path for the corrupted state.\", \"example\": \"(async () => { const currentContent = await this.skillManager.readSkillContent(skill); this.backups.set(skillSlug, { content: currentContent, ... }); })();\"}, {\"severity\": \"critical\", \"file\": \"src/tools/core-tools.ts:63,77 + src/tools/skill-tools.ts:56\", \"issue\": \"Interface mismatch between self-managed SkillManager and @zcrystal/evo SkillManager — local SkillManager returns `Result<Skill[]>` from getSkills() and `Result<Skill>` from getSkill() with .ok/.data fields, while evo SkillManager returns bare values. core-tools zcrystal_skills() checks result.ok but task-tools expects bare values; tool wrappers use inconsistent calling conventions for the same state.skillManager.\", \"example\": \"state.skillManager.getSkills() in core-tools checks result.ok — but state.skillManager is the local skill-manager.ts instance that doesn't use Result types at all; getSkills() returns Skill[] directly, making .ok always undefined.\"}, {\"severity\": \"critical\", \"file\": \"src/honcho-client.ts:203-242\", \"issue\": \"Safety gate `_checkAndRecordWrite` deduplicates only on first 200 chars of first message, so a burst of messages with the same opening 200 chars but different bodies will pass deduplication and consume multiple daily budget slots. Also the hash is not a real hash — slice(0,200) + replace + trim is not stable across semantically identical content.\", \"example\": \"const hash = content.slice(0, 200).replace(/\\\\s+/g, ' ').trim() — two messages with identical first 200 chars but completely different bodies are deduplicated incorrectly.\"}, {\"severity\": \"critical\", \"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:292-320\", \"issue\": \"evolveSkill runs async operations over ALL candidates sequentially inside Promise.all, but each candidate evaluation (evaluateWithLLM) calls honcho.ask() which is a network I/O — no concurrency limit; a skill with 10 candidates will fire 10 simultaneous Honcho API calls.\", \"example\": \"const scoredPromises = allVariants.map(v => this.scoreCandidateAsync(skill, variant, index)); await Promise.all(scoredPromises) — no batching or concurrency control.\"}, {\"severity\": \"critical\", \"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:266-282\", \"issue\": \"Two-phase evolution (prepareEvolution / commitEvolution) can leave recoveryPoints orphaned — if commitEvolution is never called after prepareEvolution, the recovery point stays in the Map indefinitely. No expiry or cleanup mechanism.\", \"example\": \"prepareEvolution() adds to recoveryPoints but only commitEvolution or rollbackEvolution removes it.\"}, {\"severity\": \"critical\", \"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:223-239\", \"issue\": \"Auto-trigger checkAutoTrigger fires evolution for EVERY skill trace when traces.length >= MIN_TRACES_FOR_ANALYSIS and successRate < threshold — since evolveSkill internally runs Promise.all over all candidates with network I/O, a burst of low-performing traces can spawn many simultaneous evolutions with no debounce or concurrency guard.\", \"example\": \"recordTrace() → checkAutoTrigger() → evolveSkill(skill) (fire-and-forget) — 5 low-scoring traces in rapid succession can trigger 5 parallel evolveSkill calls for the same skill.\"}]", "improvements": "[{\"file\": \"src/index.ts:57-601\", \"issue\": \"PluginState god object with 25+ properties violates single-responsibility — all subsystems (router, honcho, skillManager, selfEvolution, toolHub, skillGenerator, etc.) are instantiated at once and held in one flat object. The register() function is 600 lines with tool registration, interval setup, and hook registration all in one block.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/index.ts:603\", \"issue\": \"Hardcoded tool count in log message: 'Tools registered: 95' — this is a static string not derived from actual registration count, so it will always say 95 even if fewer tools are registered.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/honcho-client.ts\", \"issue\": \"Inconsistent error handling: ensureWorkspace/peer/session suppress all errors and return false/null, but addMessages/addMessages are fire-and-forget with no retry. learnFromUser writes to a separate session 'zcrystal-traces' but addMessages checks safety gate only on first message, not all messages.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/honcho-client.ts:268-295\", \"issue\": \"getMessages() returns a single synthetic 'context' message instead of actual conversation messages — this means core-tools zcrystal_search (which uses honcho.search, not getMessages) is the only way to retrieve conversation history, but honcho.search() also has wrong return type annotation (SearchResult[] vs actual API shape).\"}, {\"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:116-144\", \"issue\": \"MUTATION_RULESclarity rule applies `content.replace(/\\\\./g, '.').replace(/([a-z])\\\\./g, '$1. ')` — the first replace is a no-op (replacing '.' with '.'), making the whole clarity mutation effectively just appending '\\n\\nProvide specific examples.' to short content. The mutation is meaningless for most content.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts\", \"issue\": \"Pending evaluations Map never cleaned up in error paths — if evaluateWithLLM throws before returning, the promise stays in pendingEvaluations; no size limit or LRU eviction on the Map.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/skill-manager.ts:91-92\", \"issue\": \"Frontmatter parser only handles single-quoted YAML values — metadata JSON with double quotes won't parse correctly; content with colons in values will break the simple regex extraction.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/skill-manager.ts:156-169\", \"issue\": \"writeSkillContent re-parses using basename(skill.filePath, '.md') which loses directory information for nested skill paths; if a skill file is at /path/to/skill/SKILL.md, the re-parsed skill slug will be 'SKILL' not the original subdirectory name.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/tools/\", \"issue\": \"Duplicated/redundant tool functionality: zcrystal_skill_versions (skill-tools.ts:160) and zcrystal_version_list (skill-tools.ts:44) both call the same router.getSkillVersions; zcrystal_skill_rollback and zcrystal_version_rollback duplicate the same rollback operation; zcrystal_adapter_* tools (workflow-tools) duplicate what skillManager already does.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/tools/system-tools.ts:67-75\", \"issue\": \"zcrystal_hook_register always returns errResult with 'use hook_dispatch' — it is registered as a tool but has no functional implementation, misleading users into thinking hook registration is available.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/tools/skill-tools.ts:93\", \"issue\": \"zcrystal_indexer_rebuild returns errResult('not yet implemented') — it is a registered tool that tells users it doesn't work, rather than being conditional or returning a helpful message with actual workaround steps.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/index.ts:82-106\", \"issue\": \"fts5Search and fts5Stats make raw fetch calls with no response schema validation — they assume jsonrpc 2.0 response structure with no timeout/resilience config beyond a single 10s AbortController; if the FTS5 MCP URL is misconfigured or returns non-JSON, the error message is just 'FTS5 search failed' with no diagnostic info.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:540-542\", \"issue\": \"The scheduler's runScheduledEvolution loops skills sequentially and calls evolveSkill (which is async and does full candidate generation/scoring) for each skill — with 20 skills and 10 candidates each, this could mean 200 sequential Honcho API calls in a single scheduler tick, with no parallelization or progress tracking.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:550\", \"issue\": \"Chinese characters in log: 'No skills需要 scheduled evolution' — encoding issue or incorrect string concatenation making the log message unreadable.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/tools/proactive-tools.ts:188-195\", \"issue\": \"zcrystal_predict hardcodes confidence: 0.6 with no actual prediction logic — returns placeholder confidence and maps suggestions[].reason which may not exist on the actual suggestion type.\"}]", "working_well": "[{\"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts\", \"concept\": \"Closed-loop verification with rollback — AppliedCandidate tracking, DEGRADATION_THRESHOLD rollback, and backup-based rollback in performRollback() is a solid architectural pattern for production self-evolution systems.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/honcho-client.ts:172-200\", \"concept\": \"Safety gates design — daily write limit + content deduplication is the right approach to prevent runaway feedback loops; the MAX_WRITES_PER_DAY = 1000 is a reasonable default.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/skill-manager.ts:57-83\", \"concept\": \"Recursive skill discovery with max-depth guard — prevents infinite loops on circular symlinks or deep directory trees, with depth=3 being a sensible default.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:40-53\", \"concept\": \"GEPA-inspired mutation rules with 4 strategies (clarity/structure/examples/constraints) — the multi-variant generation gives evolution candidates good diversity to explore.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/config.ts:17\", \"concept\": \"Using os.tmpdir() instead of hardcoded /tmp/zcrystal — the FIX comment correctly identifies platform portability issues.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/index.ts:56-69\", \"concept\": \"Plugin entry uses openclaw/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry definePluginEntry pattern — this correctly follows OpenClaw's plugin registration contract with proper api.registerTool/Command/Hook calls.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/honcho-client.ts:41-58\", \"concept\": \"Auth helper getHeaders() is clean and centralized — all API calls go through it, making API key injection straightforward.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:227-258\", \"concept\": \"Two-phase evolution (prepare/commit) with recovery pointer — Pattern 9 (Task Decomposition) is well-implemented; creating recovery point before mutation and only updating on commit is the correct sequence.\"}, {\"file\": \"src/tools/index.ts\", \"concept\": \"Tool registration is cleanly split by domain (core/task/skill/workflow/system/proactive) — each module has a single responsibility, making the codebase navigable despite overall complexity.\"}]", "priority_fixes": "[{\"priority\": 1, \"file\": \"src/index.ts:177-204\", \"fix\": \"Add unregister/teardown function that calls clearInterval on both heartbeat and proactive intervals. Expose a dispose() on the plugin or hook into OpenClaw's lifecycle. At minimum, store interval IDs in state and clear them if state is being replaced.\"}, {\"priority\": 2, \"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:223-239\", \"fix\": \"Add debounce/throttle to checkAutoTrigger — use a Set of skills currently evolving to prevent concurrent evolveSkill calls for the same skill; add a cooldown period between auto-trigger invocations for the same slug.\"}, {\"priority\": 3, \"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:375-385\", \"fix\": \"Make backup creation synchronous before the skill write, not fire-and-forget. Block the write on the backup completing. Add retry logic if backup write fails.\"}, {\"priority\": 4, \"file\": \"src/tools/core-tools.ts:63,77 + src/tools/skill-tools.ts:56\", \"fix\": \"Unify the SkillManager interface between local skill-manager.ts and @zcrystal/evo. Pick one convention (Result<T> or bare T) and make all tool wrappers use it consistently. The local SkillManager returns bare values, not Result objects — the .ok/.data checks in core-tools are dead code.\"}, {\"priority\": 5, \"file\": \"src/honcho-client.ts:203-242\", \"fix\": \"Use a proper hash (e.g., crypto.createHash) of the full content instead of slice(0,200). Apply deduplication check to all messages in the batch, not just the first.\"}, {\"priority\": 6, \"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:292-320\", \"fix\": \"Add concurrency limiting to evolveSkill candidate evaluation — use a semaphore or Promise pool (max 3 concurrent evaluations) instead of Promise.all over all candidates simultaneously.\"}, {\"priority\": 7, \"file\": \"src/index.ts\", \"fix\": \"Extract the register() block into smaller functions: registerTools(state), setupIntervals(state), registerHooks(state). Convert the module-level state into a proper Plugin class with lifecycle methods (init/dispose).\"}, {\"priority\": 8, \"file\": \"src/self-evolution.ts:266-282\", \"fix\": \"Add expiry/cleanup for orphaned recoveryPoints — track creation timestamp and clean up points older than 24h on initialize(), or clear them when the skill is next successfully written.\"}]", "report_metadata": "{\"repo\": \"zcrystal-plugin\", \"reviewed_files\": [\"src/index.ts\", \"src/honcho-client.ts\", \"src/self-evolution.ts\", \"src/skill-manager.ts\", \"src/tools/index.ts\", \"src/tools/core-tools.ts\", \"src/tools/task-tools.ts\", \"src/tools/skill-tools.ts\", \"src/tools/workflow-tools.ts\", \"src/tools/system-tools.ts\", \"src/tools/proactive-tools.ts\", \"src/config.ts\", \"src/types.ts\", \"src/user-analyzer.ts\", \"src/fts5-bridge.ts\"], \"lines_reviewed\": 2500}", "report_format": "json"}
blocks.name: StructuredOutput

blocks.text:
## ZCrystal Plugin Code Review Report

**Files reviewed:** 15 source files (~2,500 lines)  
**Format:** JSON (structured output above)

---

### 🔴 Critical Issues (8)

| # | File | Issue |
|---|------|-------|
| 1 | `src/index.ts:177-204` | `setInterval` timers for heartbeat/proactive checks **never cleared** — hot-reload or re-registration orphans old intervals that keep firing with stale state references |
| 2 | `src/index.ts:60-69` | Module-level `let state` singleton gets **overwritten without cleanup** on re-registration — running timers + in-flight async ops from old state persist, new tool closures capture new state creating a split-brain situation |
| 3 | `src/self-evolution.ts:375-385, 640-662` | **Fire-and-forget IIFE** for backup creation: backup write runs detached from skill write; if write fails, there's no rollback path because backup may not have been written |
| 4 | `src/tools/core-tools.ts:63,77` | `SkillManager` **interface mismatch** — `core-tools` calls `.ok`/`.data` on local SkillManager (which returns bare `Skill[]` not `Result<T>`), so `.ok` is always `undefined` and the data access is dead code |
| 5 | `src/honcho-client.ts:203-242` | Safety gate **deduplication uses slice(0,200)** of first message only — two messages with identical opening 200 chars but different bodies bypass deduplication; hash is not a real hash, just string slice |
| 6 | `src/self-evolution.ts:292-320` | `Promise.all` over all candidates fires **unbounded concurrent Honcho API calls** — 10 candidates = 10 simultaneous network requests with no concurrency limit |
| 7 | `src/self-evolution.ts:266-282` | `recoveryPoints` **Map grows unbounded** — `prepareEvolution` adds entries, only `rollbackEvolution` removes them; orphaned entries from partial evolutions never cleaned up |
| 8 | `src/self-evolution.ts:223-239` | Auto-trigger `checkAutoTrigger` fires `evolveSkill` **fire-and-forget** per trace — rapid low-performing traces can spawn multiple parallel evolutions for the same skill with no debounce |

---

### 🟡 Improvements (15)

| # | File | Issue |
|---|------|-------|
| 1 | `src/index.ts:57-601` | 600-line `register()` function + 25-property `PluginState` god object — violates single-responsibility |
| 2 | `src/index.ts:603` | Hardcoded `'Tools registered: 95'` — static string, always says 95 regardless of actual count |
| 3 | `src/honcho-client.ts` | Inconsistent error handling — some methods suppress all errors and return false, others have fire-and-forget writes |
| 4 | `src/honcho-client.ts:268-295` | `getMessages()` returns synthetic "context" message instead of actual conversation messages |
| 5 | `src/self-evolution.ts:116-144` | `clarity` mutation rule: `replace(/\./g, '.')` is a **no-op** (replaces '.' with itself) |
| 6 | `src/self-evolution.ts` | `pendingEvaluations` Map has **no size limit or LRU eviction** |
| 7 | `src/skill-manager.ts:91-92` | Frontmatter parser only handles **single-quoted YAML** — breaks on standard double-quoted JSON |
| 8 | `src/skill-manager.ts:156-169` | `writeSkillContent` re-parses with `basename()` losing **nested path info** — slug becomes 'SKILL' not original subdirectory name |
| 9 | `src/tools/` | **Tool duplication** — `zcrystal_skill_versions` and `zcrystal_version_list` both call `router.getSkillVersions` |
| 10 | `src/tools/system-tools.ts:67-75` | `zcrystal_hook_register` always returns error — **no functional implementation** |
| 11 | `src/tools/skill-tools.ts:93` | `zcrystal_indexer_rebuild` returns `'not yet implemented'` — actively misleads users |
| 12 | `src/index.ts:82-106` | FTS5 fetch has no response schema validation — wrong content type or non-JSON response produces unhelpful `'FTS5 search failed'` |
| 13 | `src/self-evolution.ts:540-542` | Scheduler loops **sequentially** through skills — with 20 skills × 10 candidates, 200 sequential Honcho calls per tick |
| 14 | `src/self-evolution.ts:550` | **Garbled log**: `'No skills需要 scheduled evolution'` — encoding or string concat bug |
| 15 | `src/tools/proactive-tools.ts:188-195` | `zcrystal_predict` hardcodes `confidence: 0.6` — no actual prediction logic |

---

### 🟢 Working Well (9)

| # | File | Concept |
|---|------|---------|
| 1 | `src/self-evolution.ts` | Closed-loop verification + rollback pattern (`AppliedCandidate`, `DEGRADATION_THRESHOLD`, `performRollback`) — solid production-grade self-evolution design |
| 2 | `src/honcho-client.ts:172-200` | Safety gates (daily limit 1000 + content deduplication) — correct approach to prevent feedback loops |
| 3 | `src/skill-manager.ts:57-83` | Recursive discovery with `depth > 3` guard — prevents infinite loops on symlinks |
| 4 | `src/self-evolution.ts:40-53` | GEPA-inspired 4-strategy mutation rules (clarity/structure/examples/constraints) |
| 5 | `src/config.ts:17` | `os.tmpdir()` instead of hardcoded `/tmp/zcrystal` — platform-portable |
| 6 | `src/index.ts:56-69` | Correct use of `definePluginEntry` pattern — follows OpenClaw contract |
| 7 | `src/honcho-client.ts:41-58` | `getHeaders()` centralized auth helper — all API calls go through it |
| 8 | `src/self-evolution.ts:227-258` | Two-phase evolution (prepare/commit) with recovery pointer — well-implemented Pattern 9 |
| 9 | `src/tools/index.ts` | Domain-based tool split (core/task/skill/workflow/system/proactive) — navigable despite complexity |

---

### 🔧 Priority Fixes (8)

1. **`src/index.ts:177-204`** — Store interval IDs in state, add `clearInterval` on teardown; wire into OpenClaw lifecycle
2. **`src/self-evolution.ts:223-239`** — Add `Set<string>` of currently-evolving skills to debounce concurrent evolutions
3. **`src/self-evolution.ts:375-385`** — Block backup creation before skill write; make it synchronous with retry
4. **`src/tools/core-tools.ts:63,77`** — Unify `SkillManager` interface; remove dead `.ok/.data` checks on local instance
5. **`src/honcho-client.ts:203-242`** — Use `crypto.createHash` on full content; deduplicate all messages in batch
6. **`src/self-evolution.ts:292-320`** — Add concurrency limiter (max 3 concurrent evaluations) with a simple semaphore
7. **`src/index.ts`** — Extract `register()` into `registerTools()`, `setupIntervals()`, `registerHooks()`; convert to `Plugin` class with `dispose()`
8. **`src/self-evolution.ts:266-282`** — Track `recoveryPoints` creation timestamp; clean up entries older than 24h on `initialize()`
